#> 145: 42289684 abstract             78          16
#> 146: 42289684 abstract             78          17
#> 147: 42289684 abstract             78          18
#> 148: 42289684 abstract             78          19
#> 149: 42289684    title              0           0
#>                                                                                                                                                                                                                      sentence
#>                                                                                                                                                                                                                        <char>
#>   1:                                                                                                         Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma (ATC) is a highly aggressive malignancy with limited effective clinical therapies. 
#>   2:           Photothermal therapy (PTT) and doxorubicin (DOX) chemotherapy have shown potential against ATC, yet their clinical application is hindered by limited tissue penetration and systemic toxicity, respectively. 
#>   3:                                                                                                                 In this study, we developed an innovative combinatorial strategy integrating PTT with DOX chemotherapy. 
#>   4:                                                  In vitro, this combined treatment markedly enhanced DOX uptake by ATC cells, inhibited cell proliferation, migration, and invasion, and effectively induced apoptosis. 
#>   5:                                                      Additionally, it elevated intracellular reactive oxygen species levels, exacerbated DNA damage, and downregulated the expression of angiogenesis-related proteins. 
#>  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
#> 145:                                                                                                       GSEA confirmed TGF-beta pathway enrichment, emphasizing its role in inflammation, fibrosis, and oxidative stress. 
#> 146:           Notably, SB-431,542, a TGF-beta receptor kinase inhibitor, mitigated DOX-induced apoptosis, improved cell viability, and ameliorated DOX-induced cardiotoxicity by inhibiting the phosphorylation of Smad2/3. 
#> 147: CONCLUSION: Our study identifies TGF-beta1 as a central regulator of DOX-induced cardiotoxicity and highlights SB-431,542 as a promising therapeutic agent for mitigating cardiomyocyte apoptosis by targeting Smad2/3. 
#> 148:                                                                                                   Our study suggests that TGF-beta1 may serve as a potential therapeutic target for reducing DOX-induced cardiotoxicity.
#> 149:                                                                                                                                            Identification of TGF-beta1 as a key regulator in DOX-induced cardiotoxicity.

Co-occurrence counts

Use pubtator_cooccurrence() for count-only summaries over sentence windows. The function accepts either the full context object or ctx$entities. window = 0 counts entities in the same sentence; larger windows reach across adjacent sentences within the same passage.

# entity-type pairs in the same sentence
pubtator_cooccurrence(ctx, window = 0, by = "type")
#>       type_x   type_y     n n_pmids
#>       <char>   <char> <int>   <int>
#>  1: Chemical  Disease   176      13
#>  2: Chemical     Gene    94       9
#>  3:  Disease     Gene    87      10
#>  4:     Gene     Gene    84       8
#>  5: Chemical Chemical    83      11
#>  6:  Disease  Disease    80      12
#>  7: Chemical  Species    27       7
#>  8:  Disease  Species    19       6
#>  9:     Gene  Species     6       2
#> 10:  Disease  Variant     4       1
#> 11: Chemical  Variant     4       1
#> 12:     Gene  Variant     2       1
#> 13:  Species  Species     1       1
#> 14:  Variant  Variant     1       1
# specific entity pairs, within one sentence of each other
pubtator_cooccurrence(ctx, window = 1, by = "entity")
#>        type_x identifier_x         text_x   type_y identifier_y         text_y
#>        <char>       <char>         <char>   <char>       <char>         <char>
#>   1: Chemical MESH:D004317            DOX  Disease MESH:D066126 cardiotoxicity
#>   2: Chemical            -            Rg3 Chemical            -            TSF
#>   3:  Disease MESH:D028361  mitochondrial     Gene         7416          VDAC1
#>   4: Chemical MESH:D004317             DR  Disease MESH:D066126 cardiotoxicity
#>   5: Chemical MESH:D004317    Doxorubicin  Disease MESH:D066126 cardiotoxicity
#>  ---                                                                          
#> 813:  Disease MESH:D005355       fibrosis     Gene         7040       TGF-beta
#> 814: Chemical            -     SB-431,542     Gene         7040       TGF-beta
#> 815:  Disease MESH:D066126 cardiotoxicity     Gene         7040       TGF-beta
#> 816: Chemical            -     SB-431,542  Disease MESH:D007249   inflammation
#> 817: Chemical            -     SB-431,542  Disease MESH:D005355       fibrosis
#>          n n_pmids
#>      <int>   <int>
#>   1:    38       7
#>   2:    26       1
#>   3:    15       1
#>   4:    12       1
#>   5:    11       9
#>  ---              
#> 813:     1       1
#> 814:     1       1
#> 815:     1       1
#> 816:     1       1
#> 817:     1       1

Relation networks

pubtator_network() turns contextualized PubTator relations into a directed entity network plus a lean evidence table. Nodes are specific entities keyed on their PubTator identifier when present, falling back to type:text; edges are directed PubTator relation assertions; evidence rows map each edge back to pmid, relation_id, and the shared sentence when the endpoint mentions occur in the same sentence.

rel_net <- pubtator_network(ctx)

rel_net
#> $nodes

#>   2:          TRUE                 0
#>   3:         FALSE                 1
#>   4:         FALSE                 1
#>   5:         FALSE                 1

#> 108:          TRUE                 0
#> 109:          TRUE                 0
#> 110:         FALSE                 3
#> 111:         FALSE                 6
#> 112:         FALSE                 6
#>                                                                                                                    sentence
#>                                                                                                                      <char>
#>   1:                                                                                                                   <NA>
#>   2:             Importantly, the treatment exhibited favorable biocompatibility and mitigated DOX-induced cardiotoxicity. 
#>   3:                                                                                                                   <NA>
#>   4:                                                                                                                   <NA>
#>   5:                                                                                                                   <NA>
#>  ---                                                                                                                       
#> 108: Our study suggests that TGF-beta1 may serve as a potential therapeutic target for reducing DOX-induced cardiotoxicity.
#> 109: Our study suggests that TGF-beta1 may serve as a potential therapeutic target for reducing DOX-induced cardiotoxicity.
#> 110:                                                                                                                   <NA>
#> 111:                                                                                                                   <NA>
#> 112:                                                                                                                   <NA>

puremoe takes on no graphing dependency itself, but the graph tables pass straight to a graph package such as igraph:

igraph::graph_from_data_frame(rel_net$edges, vertices = rel_net$nodes)

Developed by Jason Timm.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
